---
title: "Corpus Register"
draft: false
---

# Corpus Register

The corpus register makes the Lincoln corpus a documented scholarly object rather than a folder of source files. The canonical structured source is `corpus/corpus_manifest.json`; the publication table is generated from that source with:

```bash
npm run corpus:register
```

The generated CSV is written to `data/metadata/corpus-register.csv`.

## Scope

The register covers the existing 28-document Lincoln corpus, 1838-1865. The corpus is intentionally wide: formal public addresses, campaign speeches, debates, letters, congressional messages, legal documents, and private fragments remain in the same corpus so that register and authorship confidence can be controlled rather than hidden by selection.

```{ojs}
manifest = await FileAttachment("corpus/corpus_manifest.json").json()
docs = manifest.documents
```

## Corpus Controls

```{ojs}
periodCounts = Array.from(
  d3.rollup(docs, v => v.length, d => d.period),
  ([period, documents]) => ({period, documents})
)

registerCounts = Array.from(
  d3.rollup(docs, v => v.length, d => d.register),
  ([register, documents]) => ({register, documents})
).sort((a, b) => b.documents - a.documents)

authorshipCounts = Array.from(
  d3.rollup(docs, v => v.length, d => d.authorship),
  ([authorship, documents]) => ({authorship, documents})
)
```

### Periodization

```{ojs}
Inputs.table(periodCounts, {
  columns: ["period", "documents"],
  header: {period: "Period", documents: "Documents"}
})
```

### Register Distribution

```{ojs}
Inputs.table(registerCounts, {
  columns: ["register", "documents"],
  header: {register: "Register", documents: "Documents"}
})
```

### Authorship

```{ojs}
Inputs.table(authorshipCounts, {
  columns: ["authorship", "documents"],
  header: {authorship: "Authorship", documents: "Documents"}
})
```

## Publication Register

```{ojs}
viewof registerFilter = Inputs.select(
  ["All", ...manifest.register_enum],
  {label: "Register", value: "All"}
)

viewof periodFilter = Inputs.select(
  ["All", ...manifest.periodization_enum],
  {label: "Period", value: "All"}
)

viewof cautionOnly = Inputs.checkbox(
  ["Show only documents with risk flags"],
  {label: "Caution filter"}
)
```

```{ojs}
registerRows = docs
  .filter(d => registerFilter === "All" || d.register === registerFilter)
  .filter(d => periodFilter === "All" || d.period === periodFilter)
  .filter(d => !cautionOnly.includes("Show only documents with risk flags") || d.risk_flags.length > 0)
  .map(d => ({
    id: d.id,
    title: d.short_title,
    date: d.date,
    period: d.period,
    genre: d.genre,
    register: d.register,
    authorship: d.authorship,
    confidence: d.authorship_confidence,
    source: d.source_edition,
    url: d.source_url,
    status: d.editorial_status,
    rationale: d.inclusion_rationale,
    limitations: d.known_limitations.join("; "),
    risk: d.risk_flags.length ? d.risk_flags.join(", ") : "none"
  }))

Inputs.table(registerRows, {
  columns: [
    "id", "title", "date", "period", "genre", "register", "authorship",
    "confidence", "source", "url", "status", "rationale", "limitations", "risk"
  ],
  header: {
    id: "ID",
    title: "Document",
    date: "Date",
    period: "Period",
    genre: "Genre",
    register: "Register",
    authorship: "Authorship",
    confidence: "Auth. Conf.",
    source: "Source Edition",
    url: "Source URL",
    status: "Editorial Status",
    rationale: "Inclusion Rationale",
    limitations: "Known Limitations",
    risk: "Risk Flags"
  },
  format: {
    confidence: x => x.toFixed(2),
    id: id => htl.html`<a href="../../corpus/text/${id}.html">${id}</a>`,
    url: url => htl.html`<a href="${url}">source</a>`
  },
  rows: 30
})
```

## Caution Notes

The debate documents (`doc_006a` through `doc_006g`) have the most important provenance caution. They are Lincoln-primary rather than Lincoln-sole because the texts depend on newspaper transcription, and annotation is limited to Lincoln turns.

The First Inaugural (`doc_009`) includes Seward-drafted or Seward-revised passages. Authorship-sensitive analysis should isolate Lincoln-sole sentences where possible.

Private fragments and memoranda have high interpretive value but limited audience context. They should be used to test whether public rhetoric aligns with private reasoning, not as frequency evidence equivalent to formal addresses.

Legal documents intentionally suppress metaphor. Their flatness is part of the register evidence, especially for the Emancipation Proclamation documents.

See [Register Definitions](../../skills/corpus_register.md) for the analytical implications of each register.
